 {"id":"bd-1mbs","title":"Editor: Ctrl+V image paste + drag/drop attachment","description":"# Goal
Support image attachment UX in interactive mode:
- Ctrl+V to paste an image from clipboard.
- Drag/drop a file onto the terminal to attach.

# Legacy Behavior
- On Ctrl+V, legacy tries to read clipboard image; if present, it writes to a temp file and inserts the file path at cursor.
  - See `legacy_pi_mono_code/pi-mono/packages/coding-agent/src/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.ts` (`handleClipboardImagePaste`).

# Required Behavior
- Ctrl+V:
  - If clipboard contains an image, write it to a temp file (png/jpg based on mime if possible).
  - Insert the resulting path into the editor at the cursor.
  - If clipboard has no image or access denied, do nothing silently.

- Drag/drop:
  - Many terminals paste a quoted or escaped file path. Ensure we normalize/sanitize so that subsequent file reference processing can resolve it.

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users can attach an image without manually saving a file.
- [ ] No crashes when clipboard access is unavailable.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-1mh","title":"Update session picker to use SQLite index","description":"# Update session picker to use SQLite index

## Goal
Replace filesystem walking in session picker with fast SQLite index queries.

## Current Implementation (src/session_picker.rs)
Currently scans filesystem:
```rust
// Slow: walks all session directories
let sessions = walkdir::WalkDir::new(&sessions_dir)
    .into_iter()
    .filter_map(|e| e.ok())
    .filter(|e| e.path().extension() == Some("jsonl"))
    .collect();
```

## New Implementation
```rust
use crate::session_index::SessionIndex;

impl SessionPicker {
    pub fn load_sessions(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
        let index = SessionIndex::open_default()?;
        index.list_sessions(cwd)
    }
}
```

## UI Changes
- Display indexed metadata directly (no file reads needed)
- Show message count from index
- Show session name from index
- Faster render (no filesystem I/O)

## Fallback Behavior
If index unavailable or empty:
```rust
fn load_sessions_with_fallback(cwd: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<SessionMeta>> {
    // Try index first
    if let Ok(index) = SessionIndex::open_default() {
        if let Ok(sessions) = index.list_sessions(cwd) {
            if !sessions.is_empty() {
                return Ok(sessions);
            }
        }
    }
    
    // Fallback to filesystem (and populate index)
    let sessions = load_sessions_from_filesystem(cwd)?;
    // Optionally trigger reindex in background
    Ok(sessions)
}
```

## Dependencies
- bd-3nz (indexing must work first)

## Testing
- Test: Session picker shows indexed sessions
- Test: Picker falls back to filesystem if needed
- Test: Performance improved (measure time)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Picker queries index by default
- [ ] Fallback to filesystem works
- [ ] UI displays index metadata
- [ ] Noticeably faster for 50+ sessions","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented

- Add unit tests for core CLI/main.rs logic without mocks (real temp dirs + config fixtures) and with detailed logging.

- Model selection + thinking resolution (select_model_and_thinking) across provider/model edge cases.

- Logs include inputs, derived values, and assertion context.

-{"id":"bd-1msi","title":"E2E: Azure OpenAI provider — live streaming + deployment matrix","description":"Create true live E2E integration tests for Azure OpenAI using real credentials and deployment config.

Scope:
- Load AZURE_OPENAI_API_KEY, AZURE_OPENAI_RESOURCE, AZURE_OPENAI_DEPLOYMENT (plus optional API version) from configured auth/model sources.
- Run short, cost-capped prompts (<30 output tokens where possible) for:
  1) basic text response
  2) streaming event order and completion semantics
  3) tool call emission/roundtrip when model supports tools
  4) error-path sanity (invalid deployment or missing config should skip/fail with actionable diagnostics)
- Emit detailed JSONL logs and artifacts per run:
  - request metadata (resource/deployment redacted)
  - latency breakdown (connect, first token, full completion)
  - usage/token metrics when available
  - stop_reason and event timeline

Requirements:
- No VCR playback in this suite; must perform real network calls behind CI_E2E_TESTS=1.
- Skip cleanly (not failure) when Azure credentials/config are absent.
- Keep prompts short to minimize spend.

- Output a deterministic artifact index and the updated matrix file.

# Deliverables
- Updated `docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md` (or equivalent) with clear status and next actions.
- List of gaps mapped to bead ids (existing or newly created).

- Matrix clearly shows coverage status per module and excludes unapproved mocks.

Next: update coverage matrix doc when ready.","created_at":"2026-02-04T05:14:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1no3","title":"Phase 3: Differential Test Runner (compare TS vs Rust output)","description":"# Phase 3: Differential Test Runner (compare TS vs Rust output)

## Purpose
This is the CORE of the conformance system. The differential test runner:
1. Takes an extension path and a mock spec
2. Runs it through the TS runtime (via Bun subprocess)
3. Runs it through the Rust runtime (via library call)
4. Compares the outputs
5. Reports PASS (identical) or FAIL (with diff)

## Why A Runner (Not Just Tests)
Individual test functions are fine for small numbers of extensions. But we have 200+ extensions to test. The runner automates:
- Extension discovery from the validated manifest
- Mock spec selection (per-extension or default)
- Parallel execution (both runtimes concurrently)
- Structured output comparison
- Report generation

## Runner Architecture

```
ConformanceRunner
  |
  +-- for each extension in manifest:
  |     |
  |     +-- Run TS harness (Bun subprocess) -> ts_output.json
  |     +-- Run Rust harness (library call) -> rust_output.json
  |     +-- Compare(ts_output, rust_output) -> diff_result
  |     +-- Record result
  |
  +-- Generate report: {total, passed, failed, skipped, diffs: [...]}
```

## Comparison Strategy
Not just byte-equality. Semantic comparison that:
- Ignores key ordering in JSON objects
- Handles numeric precision differences (f64 vs TS number)
- Normalizes string whitespace where appropriate
- Groups differences by category (registration, event, hostcall)
- Provides human-readable diff output

## Output
- Per-extension: PASS/FAIL with detailed diff
- Aggregate: summary report with pass rate per tier
- Machine-readable: JSON report for CI
- Human-readable: markdown report for review","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:50.307953690Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:53.485560824Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:34:53.485496845Z","close_reason":"Differential test runner implemented in tests/ext_conformance_diff.rs. Runner: discovers extensions from VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json, runs TS oracle (Bun + load_extension.ts), runs Rust runtime (QuickJS), compares outputs with diff_snapshots(). All 60 official extensions pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1no3","depends_on_id":"bd-3odv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:50.327770840Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":311,"issue_id":"bd-1no3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN DECISION: Why Rust integration test (not standalone binary)?

1. CARGO TEST INTEGRATION: Each extension as a #[test] function = automatic parallelism, filtering, and CI integration.
2. LIBRARY ACCESS: Can call Rust extension runtime directly without subprocess overhead.
3. EXISTING PATTERN: conformance_fixtures.rs already uses this pattern with fixture_test! macro.
4. REPORTING: cargo test --format json gives structured output for CI.

The TS side runs as a Bun subprocess because we cannot embed the TS runtime in Rust. This is fine -- the subprocess produces JSON on stdout, which is trivial to parse. The overhead is ~50ms per subprocess spawn, acceptable for 200 extensions.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:27:17Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-np43","title":"Task: Integrate extension commands into interactive processor","description":"# Task: Integrate Extension Commands into Interactive Processor

## Objective

Modify the interactive command processor to recognize and route extension-registered slash commands.

## Background

The interactive module processes user input, including slash commands. Extension commands must be integrated:
1. When user types /foo, check if foo is an extension command
2. If so, route to extension handler instead of built-in processing
3. Handle the result and display output

## Implementation

### Add Extension Command Check

```rust
async fn process_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_command(input);
    
    // Check built-in commands first (they take precedence)
    match cmd {
        "help" => return self.show_help(),
        "clear" => return self.clear_history(),
        "model" => return self.change_model(args),
        _ => {}
    }
    
    // Check extension commands
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        let is_ext_cmd = ext_commands.iter().any(|c| {
            c.name == cmd || c.aliases.contains(&cmd.to_string())
        });
        
        if is_ext_cmd {
            return self.execute_extension_command(cmd, args).await;
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow!("Unknown command: /{}", cmd))
}
```

### Execute Extension Command

```rust
async fn execute_extension_command(&mut self, name: &str, args: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let runtime = self.extension_runtime.as_ref()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("No extension runtime"))?;
    
    let result = runtime.execute_command(name, args).await?;
    
    // Display output if any
    if let Some(output) = result.output {
        self.display_output(&output)?;
    }
    
    // Append messages if any
    if let Some(messages) = result.messages {
        for msg in messages {
            self.conversation.push(msg);
        }
    }
    
    if result.continue_loop { Ok(CommandAction::Continue) } else { Ok(CommandAction::Exit) }
}
```

### Update Help Command

```rust
async fn show_help(&self) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    println!("Built-in commands:");
    println!("  /help    - Show this help");
    println!("  /clear   - Clear conversation");
    
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        if !ext_commands.is_empty() {
            println!("\nExtension commands:");
            for cmd in ext_commands {
                let usage = cmd.usage.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
                println!("  /{:<8} - {} {}", cmd.name, cmd.description, usage);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(CommandAction::Continue)
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (7 cases)
1. test_builtin_commands_still_work
2. test_extension_command_recognized
3. test_extension_command_executed_via_js
4. test_output_displayed_correctly
5. test_unknown_command_returns_error
6. test_builtin_commands_take_precedence
7. test_extension_aliases_work

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/interactive_commands.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="artifacts/interactive_commands_e2e.jsonl"
mkdir -p artifacts

log() {
    echo "{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"event\":\"$1\",\"data\":$2}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log "test_start" '{"suite":"interactive_commands"}'

# Create test extension
mkdir -p /tmp/test_interactive_ext
cat > /tmp/test_interactive_ext/index.js << 'EOF'
export function activate(ctx) {
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "echo",
        description: "Echo arguments back",
        aliases: ["e"],
        handler: async (args) => ({ output: args || "(empty)" }),
    });
}
EOF

# Test with tmux for interactive mode
if command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
    SESSION="pi_test_$$"
    tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION" "pi --extension /tmp/test_interactive_ext"
    sleep 2
    
    # Type /help and capture
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/help" Enter
    sleep 1
    HELP_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "help_output" "{\"contains_echo\":$(echo "$HELP_OUTPUT" | grep -q "echo" && echo true || echo false)}"
    
    # Type /echo test
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/echo hello world" Enter
    sleep 1
    ECHO_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "echo_output" "{\"output\":\"$ECHO_OUTPUT\"}"
    
    # Type /e (alias)
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/e alias test" Enter
    sleep 1
    
    # Cleanup
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/quit" Enter
    sleep 1
    tmux kill-session -t "$SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log "test_end" '{"status":"pass"}'
else
    log "test_skip" '{"reason":"tmux not available"}'
fi

echo "Interactive commands E2E test complete. Logs: $LOG_FILE"
```

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-ie1g (execute_command)
- Depends on: bd-2qrn (get_registered_commands)
- Part of: bd-20vx (Slash Command Handler feature)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Extension commands recognized by processor
- [ ] Built-in commands take precedence
- [ ] Extension commands appear in /help
- [ ] Results displayed correctly
- [ ] Aliases work
- [ ] 7 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes with JSONL logging","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:03:18.904597311Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:46.301657257Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:21:46.301593358Z","close_reason":"All implemented: list_commands() queries registered commands from JS snapshot. execute_command() at extensions.rs:4685 routes to __pi_execute_command in JS. dispatch_extension_command() in interactive.rs:5578 integrates with interactive processor. Extension commands appear in autocomplete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-np43","depends_on_id":"bd-20vx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:12:06.872885962Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-np43","depends_on_id":"bd-2qrn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:03:29.518027210Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-np43","depends_on_id":"bd-ie1g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T20:03:26.028366357Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ocv","title":"Unit tests: error type conversions + display invariants","description":"# Goal
Cover `src/error.rs` with unit tests for constructors, From conversions, and user-facing Display messages.

# Scope / Deliverables
- `Error::config/session/provider/auth/tool/validation/extension/api` constructors.
- `From<std::io::Error>`, `From<serde_json::Error>`, `From<sqlmodel_core::Error>`, `From<asupersync::sync::LockError>` mappings.
- Display formatting includes context (provider/tool names, paths).
- Ensure errors are stable for snapshot/log comparisons.

# No‑Mock Rule
- Use real error types and real error values (temp files, malformed JSON, sqlite errors).

# Acceptance
- 12+ unit tests covering all variants and conversions.
- Display strings include expected context and are non-empty.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:26:04.321594937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:25:19.168939132Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T19:37:54.716952566Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ocv","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-ocv","depends_on_id":"bd-3ml","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":113,"issue_id":"bd-ocv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes:
- Include a test that wraps an io::Error from temp file access and asserts the Display prefix `IO error:`.
- Cover LockError::Cancelled -> Error::Aborted mapping and LockError::Poisoned -> session error.
- Ensure provider/tool errors include provider/tool names in Display strings.","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:27:02Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ofcj","title":"Expand proven Pi extension coverage (research→conformance)","description":"# Goal
Dramatically expand the set of Pi extensions that are **proven to work unmodified** across all major extension shapes (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates, packages).

# Outcome
A curated, version‑pinned catalog with conformance + performance gates that prove compatibility, reliability, and speed.

# Scope
Research → selection → acquisition → conformance → performance → documentation → refresh workflow.

# Non‑Goals
Do not modify third‑party extension source code; we only vendor exact upstream artifacts and validate them.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"WindyCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:34.843067957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T18:31:46.156756389Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":317,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The Pi extension ecosystem is spread across npm, GitHub, and template/skill packs, and current conformance coverage only proves a limited subset.

Reasoning: Proving that popular extensions run unmodified reduces maintenance and increases user trust; this requires a full pipeline from research to testing to publication.

Considerations: Artifacts must remain byte-for-byte upstream, version-pinned, and validated with correctness + performance + reliability gates.

Exit criteria: A curated catalog and test reports exist, all selected extensions pass or are explicitly excluded with rationale.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:52Z"},{"id":386,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"SCALE TARGET (USER REQUIREMENT)

Given the ecosystem size (OpenClaw/marketplaces + GitHub/npm), the “proven unmodified” set must be large enough to be convincing.

Target
- Tier-1 corpus: >= 200 extensions (true Pi extensions, unmodified)
- Tier-0 baseline: official pi-mono examples remain must-pass

Evidence requirements
- Deterministic conformance + perf/reliability gates with JSONL logs + diff artifacts.
- Offline/fixture mode in CI; live refresh is opt-in.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:18:30Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-ogdk","title":"Create E2E test runner script with logging + cost tracking","description":"Create a shell script (tests/run_e2e.sh) that: (1) Sources API keys from ~/.pi/agent/models.json (or env vars). (2) Sets CI_E2E_TESTS=1 and runs cargo test with the e2e test filter. (3) Captures detailed logs to tests/e2e_results/<timestamp>/. (4) For each provider: logs pass/fail, timing, token usage, estimated cost. (5) Produces a summary table at the end. (6) Returns exit code 0 only if all available providers pass. (7) Has --provider=<name> flag to test a single provider. (8) Has --record flag to record VCR cassettes from live calls.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:20.101455859Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:29:52.087839599Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-18j1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:37.507645185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-277x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:23.498965031Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-2q00","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:23.193042421Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-3ane","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:38.288144149Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-3c0e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:37.701201862Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-3kgb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:38.094465254Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-3l83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:23.891762414Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-vg3u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:37.898830876Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","depends_on_id":"bd-yqfx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:37.310302534Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":550,"issue_id":"bd-ogdk","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Runner contract: discover providers dynamically, execute only configured live providers by default, output per-provider verdict + latency + token/cost summary, and write normalized artifact index for CI.","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:29:52Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-ovmd","title":"Create E2E integration test harness with detailed logging","description":"Build a shared test harness in tests/e2e/ (or tests/integration/) that: (1) Loads API keys from ~/.pi/agent/models.json at runtime. (2) Provides helper functions for each provider (create_anthropic_provider, create_openai_provider, etc.). (3) Includes detailed tracing/logging for every API call — request URL, headers (redacted keys), request body size, response status, timing, token usage. (4) Has a skip mechanism: if an API key is missing, the test is skipped (not failed). (5) Uses short prompts to minimize costs (e.g. 'Say just the word hello'). (6) Supports a CI_E2E_TESTS=1 env var gate so these don't run in normal cargo test. (7) Has timeout handling (30s per request). (8) Records timing metrics for each provider.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:11:23.804856186Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T17:41:07.691892986Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T17:41:07.691868380Z","close_reason":"Completed implementation in tests/common/harness.rs + tests/e2e_live_harness.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ovmd","depends_on_id":"bd-1la6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:17.554717764Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ovmd","depends_on_id":"bd-1vfi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:16.909702823Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ovmd","depends_on_id":"bd-2r3s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:13:35.000881515Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-ovmd","depends_on_id":"bd-3l83","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:17:17.221845019Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":551,"issue_id":"bd-ovmd","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Harness contract: one shared provider-discovery gate, unified timeout/retry behavior, structured per-test context logging, and strict secret redaction before artifact write.","created_at":"2026-02-06T17:29:54Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-p2l","title":"Compaction pipeline integration tests (summary + file ops + rehydrate)","description":"# Goal
Add **compaction pipeline integration tests** covering summary generation, file ops, and rehydration.

# Scope
- Exercise compaction with/without prior summaries and branch splits.
- Validate file operation seeding, summary insertion, and rehydrate behavior.
- Assert token accounting and stop reasons in compaction flows.

# Logging / Artifacts
- JSONL logs per bd-4u9 with compaction inputs, outputs, and summary hashes.
- Capture session JSONL before/after compaction for diffing.

# Tests
- Use deterministic sessions and VCR playback where provider calls are needed.
- Include failure-path tests (missing entries, malformed summaries).

# Acceptance Criteria
- Compaction integration tests are deterministic and cover core + edge cases.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:15:47.685787185Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T03:21:41.222906231Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T03:21:41.222844416Z","close_reason":"Completed: compaction pipeline integration tests (summary + file ops + rehydrate); cargo test passes","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-p2l","depends_on_id":"bd-102","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p2l","depends_on_id":"bd-1pf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-p2l","depends_on_id":"bd-26s","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:51:46.843447341Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":182,"issue_id":"bd-p2l","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Test compaction end-to-end: compute cut point, summarize via VCR, insert compaction entry, and rehydrate messages for provider context. Validate file ops tracking (read/edited/written), summary reuse, and branch summary behavior.","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:19:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-po15","title":"Epic: Expand Pi extension corpus (unmodified compatibility)","description":"Expand to a 200+ real-world extension corpus (OpenClaw + GitHub/web) and prove unmodified compatibility with deterministic, unfakable conformance + perf/reliability evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:17.277785998Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:12:35.917471081Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compatibility","conformance","extensions","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-po15","depends_on_id":"bd-25q","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:17.309099972Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-po15","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.659610047Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":248,"issue_id":"bd-po15","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background
- Current extension sample is 16 official examples in docs/extension-sample.json (pi-mono commit df5b0f...). This is intentionally small and not representative of the full ecosystem.
- Goal from EXTENSIONS.md: run legacy extensions unmodified, avoid per-extension exceptions, keep perf overhead low (<2ms p95/tool call), and keep shims deterministic and auditable.
- Recent perf baselines captured in BENCHMARKS.md (bd-1pb) show where we are now; this epic expands the coverage set while keeping perf + reliability evidence strong.

Problem Statement
We need to dramatically increase the size and scope of the extension corpus we can prove works correctly without modification. That means moving beyond official examples into the most popular community extensions, across all shapes/sizes (tool-only, commands, event hooks, UI, providers, WASM, MCP, multi-file, deps-heavy).

Success Criteria
- A tiered extension corpus (Tier-1 must-pass + Tier-2 stretch) derived from explicit popularity/coverage criteria.
- All Tier-1 extensions run unmodified in PiJS/WASM tiers with no per-extension exceptions.
- Conformance + perf evidence at scale (fixtures + scenario suites + benchmarks) that can be regenerated deterministically.
- Clear catalog manifests with pinned sources, checksums, and provenance for every extension in the corpus.

Non-Goals
- Do not mutate or fork extension sources; if it requires changes, it is a gap in our shims/runtime, not in the extension.

Dependencies/Links
- This epic extends bd-25q (extension compatibility proof) and integrates with extc pipeline (bd-xgo) + conformance harness workstreams.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:11:55Z"},{"id":290,"issue_id":"bd-po15","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"SCOPE EXPANSION (2026-02-05)

Why
- The current in-repo corpus (official pi-mono examples + initial community set) is a great start, but it is not enough to prove the extension runtime is robust across the actual ecosystem.
- The ecosystem has grown fast (incl. OpenClaw/ClawHub-style marketplaces + viral adoption), so “popular extensions” now plausibly means 200+ distinct, real-world extensions.

New Targets (explicit, auditable)
- Candidate discovery: >= 1000 raw candidates across all sources.
- Candidate validation: >= 300 “true Pi extensions” (use Pi extension protocol / ExtensionAPI; not merely ‘Claude/agent tools’ with unrelated APIs).
- Tiered corpus selection:
  - Tier-1 (MUST PASS): >= 200 extensions representing what people actually run day-to-day.
  - Tier-2 (STRETCH): additional long-tail / niche extensions with unique API surface.
  - Tier-0 (ALWAYS): official pi-mono examples remain must-pass.

Non-negotiable constraints
- Unmodified: no per-extension source edits; any breakage is fixed in the runtime/shims/pipeline.
- Deterministic + “unfakable” evidence: conformance outputs must be reproducible and diffable (ideally: legacy-vs-rust trace diff + captured fixtures).
- Performance: the expanded set must still meet the budgets (load/event/tool overhead). Scaling cannot regress UX.

What “adding them explicitly” means
- Every selected extension is represented in a catalog/manifest with pinned provenance (repo URL + commit/tag + path), checksums, license status, and scenario linkage.
- Artifacts are archived (or referenced deterministically if redistribution is restricted) so a clean checkout can reproduce the same conformance run.

Primary workstreams in this epic
- bd-d7gn: research/selection (now includes OpenClaw + global ecosystem)
- bd-3kp3: catalog + pinning + manifests
- bd-229m: compat gap analysis at scale
- bd-7wuh: conformance + perf/reliability evidence at scale
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:02:39Z"},{"id":382,"issue_id":"bd-po15","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"NOTE: RELATIONSHIP TO bd-ofcj

- bd-ofcj is the broader “research→selection→acquire→conformance→perf→docs→refresh” epic across *all* extension shapes.
- bd-po15 is the extension-protocol-specific track (PiJS/QuickJS + unmodified compatibility) and is now a child of bd-ofcj for clarity.

Practical implication
- When in doubt, prefer aligning outputs (catalogs/manifests/tests/logs) with the bd-ofcj pipeline so users see one coherent story.","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:12:35Z"}]}
